description: Focused ntoskrnl functions for measuring whether profile-backed prototype correction improves weak Hex-Rays parameter typing.
selection_notes: Prefer real ntoskrnl exports or high-confidence internal wrappers where Hex-Rays commonly emits __int64, PVOID, void *, or _QWORD * parameters.
Use the quality report prototype_correction_stats section for applied, blocked, survivor, and negative-control counts.
Keep generated corpus output under pseudoforge_out so validation artifacts are not committed by default.

targets.reason: High-ROI creation API with several object/string/output parameters that Hex-Rays often flattens.
Single-argument object API where a wrong __int64/PVOID parameter weakens all later device-object field reasoning.
Dispatch boundary where DEVICE_OBJECT and IRP identity should feed call-site and body rewrite evidence.
Power wrapper around IofCallDriver; useful for validating profile chaining without relying on manually fixed prototypes.
Callback-registration API where opaque pointer output typing is easy to lose in raw decompiler output.
Registry callback registration spans function pointer, unicode string, opaque context, and output cookie parameters.
Notify callback API with a function pointer and remove flag that benefits from prototype-backed semantic roles.

negative_controls.selection: Functions outside the listed profile ids from the same IDA batch run.
negative_controls.expected_behavior: prototype_correction_stats.negative_controls.function_count increases while applied_parameter_type_corrections stays unchanged for those functions.

negative_controls.selection: Functions whose Hex-Rays signature is already strongly typed and profile-free.
negative_controls.expected_behavior: generic_parameter_survivors and offset_deref_survivors may be counted, but no function identity or parameter correction evidence is emitted.
